audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Peter text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for suffering in flesh, changed life, love covering, gifts/service, fiery testing, not suffering as wrongdoer.

verses.restored: Therefore, Messiah having suffered in flesh, you also arm yourselves with the same mind, because the one suffering in flesh has ceased from sin/misalignment,
so as to live the remaining time in flesh no longer for human desires but for will of Divine.
For enough is the time having passed to have worked the will of the nations, having walked in licentiousness, desires, drunkenness, revels, drinking parties, and unlawful idolatries.
In this they are surprised that you do not run together into the same flood of dissipation, and they blaspheme/slander.
They will give account to the One ready to judge living and dead.
For into this also the good news was announced to dead ones, so that they might be judged according to humans in flesh but live according to Divine in Spirit.
But the end/goal of all things has drawn near; therefore be sound-minded and sober into prayers.
Before all things, having earnest love among yourselves, because love covers a multitude of sins/misalignments.
Be hospitable to one another without grumbling.
As each received a grace-gift, serve it to one another as beautiful/good stewards of the many-colored grace of Divine.

If anyone speaks, as oracles of Divine; if anyone serves, as from strength that the Divine supplies, so that in all things the Divine may be glorified through Jesus Messiah, to whom is glory and dominion into the ages of the ages. Amen.
Beloved, do not be surprised at the fiery testing among you coming for your testing, as though a strange thing were happening to you,
but as you share in the sufferings of Messiah, rejoice, so that also in unveiling of his glory you may rejoice exulting.
If you are reproached in name of Messiah, blessed are you, because the Spirit of glory and of the Divine rests upon you.
For let none of you suffer as murderer or thief or wrongdoer or meddler.
But if as Messiah-person, let one not be ashamed, but glorify the Divine in this name.
Because the season for judgment begins from the household of the Divine; and if first from us, what is the end of those disobeying the good news of the Divine?
And if the righteous/just one is saved/healed with difficulty, where will the irreverent and sinner/misaligned appear?
Therefore also let those suffering according to will of the Divine entrust their souls/lives to a trustworthy Creator in doing good.

safety_note: 1 Peter 4 does not glorify suffering or tell people to remain in abuse. It distinguishes unjust reproach from suffering as a wrongdoer and centers doing good, prayer, love, hospitality, and entrusting life to the faithful Creator.
translation_notes: Many-colored grace preserves poikilos grace imagery.
The good-news-to-dead passage requires second-pass audit; first draft preserves ambiguity.
